data.title: AIG credit rating raised to AA- at S&P on underwriting performance, debt reduction
data.description:
S&P Global Ratings on Thursday upgraded financial strength and issuer credit ratings on American International Group's (NYSE:AIG) core subsidiaries by one level to AA- from A+, and revised the core subsidiaries outlook to stable from positive. It's AIG's first ratings upgrade from S&P in more than 10 years.

AIG (NYSE:AIG) stock gained 1.1% in Thursday afternoon trading.

The long-term counterparty credit rating on holding company AIG (NYSE:AIG) rose to A- from BBB+. That outlook was also changed to stable from positive. For context, BBB- is S&P's lowest investment-grade rating and AAA is its highest.

The upgrade reflects the company's progress and ability to "achieve consistent strong underwriting performance, comparable to that of higher-rated competitors," S&P said. It also expects AIG's (AIG) moves to minimize volatility, focus on higher-margin businesses, and streamline its expense structure "will contribute to sustainable and profitable growth."

In addition, AIG (AIG) has reduced $17.8B of debt obligations, lowering its financial leverage to under 20% by the end of 2024, compared with ~30% in 2020, S&P's release said.

data.title: Edible Garden signals margin expansion and vertical integration through NaturalShrimp acquisition
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Edible Garden AG Incorporated (EDBL) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Jim Kras reported "Q1 2025 marked a strong start to the year, reflecting the continued momentum behind our strategic transformation." Kras highlighted a realignment toward higher-margin, shelf-stable products, noting brands such as Kick. Sport Nutrition, Pickle Party, Squeezables, Pulp, and Vitamin Way are showing increased traction. He emphasized the company's expansion in national retail, strengthened relationships with major retailers like Walmart, Stop & Shop, Wakefern, ShopRite, and Berkot's Super Foods, and the leverage of patented in-store merchandising solutions.

  • Kras explained the revenue decline of $414,000 to $2.7 million was due to "our strategic decision to exit lower-margin floral and lettuce categories." He pointed to a 13% rise in cut herb sales and a 15% year-over-year increase in non-perishable portfolio revenue.

  • A milestone acquisition was announced: "One of the most transforming milestones of the year announced just yesterday was our $15.5 million acquisition from NaturalShrimp Farms. Funded through a mix of preferred equity and institutional investment, this deal strengthens our balance sheet without increasing debt and further extends our vertically integrated model."

  • Kras detailed that the Iowa aquaculture facility and patented water treatment technologies will be integrated into greenhouse operations, noting synergy with ongoing R&D efforts and potential for future nutraceutical product development.

  • Interim CFO Kostas Dafoulas stated, "For the quarter ended March 31, 2025, revenue totaled $2.7 million, a decrease of 13.2% compared to $3.1 million for the 3 months ended March 31, 2024. This decline was primarily driven by the company's strategic exit from the lower-margin floral and lettuce products." Dafoulas added, "Gross profit increased to $88,000 compared to $23,000 in the prior year period, representing an increase of approximately 283% year-over-year. Gross margin improved to 3.2%, up from 0.7% in 2024, reflecting early returns from the company's shift to higher-margin, shelf-stable product lines."

Outlook

  • Kras expressed confidence in the "focused strategy, brand momentum and an even stronger operational foundation," stating Edible Garden is "well positioned to deliver long-term value for our customers, partners and shareholders."

  • There was no explicit quantitative guidance for future quarters, but management reiterated the intent to scale higher-margin non-perishable products and leverage new infrastructure from the NaturalShrimp acquisition.

Financial Results

  • Dafoulas reported, "Non-perishable revenue... grew 15% year-over-year in the quarter, a clear indication that our innovative shelf-stable brands... are resonating with customers."

  • Cost of goods sold was $2.6 million for Q1 2025, compared to $3.1 million in Q1 2024.

  • Selling, general and administrative expenses were $3.3 million for the quarter, down from $3.9 million in the prior year period, primarily due to lower personnel costs and the roll-off of severance expenses.

  • Net loss was $3.3 million for the first three months of 2025, compared to a net loss of $4 million for the same period in 2024. The company attributed this improvement primarily to cost reductions and increased sales from higher-margin products.

Q&A

  • Anthony Vendetti, The Maxim Group: Asked about the NaturalShrimp acquisition plans and immediate/long-term synergies. Kras responded that the facility offers warehousing, supports Midwest distribution for shelf-stable products, enables R&D, and will accelerate margin impact through logistics and nutraceutical innovation. "These products that require refrigeration will be able to be housed there... The R&D aspect... I'm extremely excited about."

  • Vendetti asked about NaturalShrimp's 2024 revenues and gross margin impact. Kras said sales were nominal, and "it's not going to be margin accretive" from existing business, but expects margin benefits as the facility is integrated and utilized for Edible Garden's portfolio.

  • Vendetti inquired about the sports nutrition line's Q1 performance and future trajectory. Kras shared, "I'm ecstatic... We just gained distribution in the Midwest big box retailer. Those orders got shipped in Q2, so in April. So, they're not reflective in Q1."

  • Nick Pincus, Forest Capital: Sought further details on gross margin improvement drivers and strategic initiatives. Kras cited continued investment in people, innovation, marketing, and portfolio diversification as key levers for margin expansion, stating the company is "really evolving."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on operational synergies, gross margin expansion, and new product lines. Their tone was positive, seeking clarity on integration benefits and growth drivers.

  • Management expressed confidence and enthusiasm, particularly regarding the NaturalShrimp integration, product launches, and retail expansion. Direct statements such as "I'm ecstatic" and "we're confident in our path forward" reflect a positive and forward-looking tone.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst sentiment remained constructive, with slightly more attention on operational execution following the acquisition. Management's tone shifted from explanation of past realignment to excitement about current and future opportunities.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The Q1 2025 call shifted from discussing a proposed acquisition (Narayan Group in Q4 2024) to the completed acquisition of NaturalShrimp Farms, emphasizing U.S. vertical integration and infrastructure rather than international expansion.

  • Q1 2025 highlighted a significant improvement in gross profit and gross margin compared to Q1 2024, while total revenue declined due to the planned exit of low-margin categories.

  • Management's confidence increased, focusing on realized benefits from portfolio rationalization, cost controls, and integration of acquired assets, whereas the prior quarter emphasized transition and setup.

  • Analysts maintained their focus on margin improvement, synergies, and product pipeline, now probing the immediate impact of the NaturalShrimp acquisition.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged the decline in total revenue due to the exit of lower-margin categories, positioning it as a strategic choice for future profitability.

  • Questions arose regarding the margin accretiveness of the NaturalShrimp facility, with management clarifying that initial sales are nominal but expecting operational and margin benefits as the facility is integrated.

  • No explicit new risks or uncertainties were introduced beyond those discussed in the context of integration and execution.

Final Takeaway

Edible Garden AG Incorporated began 2025 with expanded gross margins and reduced losses, driven by a deliberate pivot to higher-margin, shelf-stable products and successful cost controls. The $15.5 million NaturalShrimp Farms acquisition provides new warehousing, R&D capabilities, and patented technologies—positioning the company for further margin expansion and operational leverage. Management expresses strong confidence in its strategy, retail relationships, and ability to build sustainable long-term value as the company executes its vertically integrated growth plan.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: British American Tobacco poised to end in green after six straight sessions of losses
data.description:
British American Tobacco (NYSE:BTI) shares were on track to snap six continuous sessions of losses as they entered the green territory on Thursday. BTI rose 1.6% to trade at $41.20 during afternoon trading.

Since May 7, the stock has continuously ended in the red until May 14, during which BTI lost nearly 9% of its value.

In the same week, the tobacco maker was further pressured after Seeking Alpha analyst Michael B. Howard downgraded it from Hold to Sell due to its high valuation and failure to grow.

“With management somewhat discredited by their unmet forecasts, new category product revenue now under 10% and valuations corrected, there is no further outsized opportunity,” Howard said.

Earlier this week, the stock slid further lower following the announcement of the U.S.-China trade deal. The tobacco and nicotine sector is widely regarded as a defensive investment, favored by investors for its consistent financial performance and resilience during economic downturns.

Demand for these products typically remains stable even in bear markets or recessions, making it a recession-resistant industry. As the trade deal calmed broader market uncertainty, BTI along with peers Philip Morris International (PM) and Altria (MO) also dived.

As per Seeking Alpha’s quant ratings, it is recommended to Hold the stock. It has a score of 3.47 out of 5, with an A+ grade for profitability but a D+ for growth. Wall Street analysts echoed the same sentiments and issued a Hold rating for BTI. However, Seeking Alpha analysts have a Buy call for the stock.

On a year-to-date basis, BTI has grown by over 12%, while in the last year it has advanced by more than 30%.

data.title: Taysha outlines expedited pivotal trial start for TSHA-102 following FDA alignment
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Taysha Gene Therapies (TSHA) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Sean P. Nolan highlighted "significant regulatory and clinical progress" in the TSHA-102 Rett syndrome program, citing written alignment from the FDA on pivotal Part B trial design and next steps to enable study initiation. He stated, "Importantly, the FDA advised us to proceed directly to submitting our pivotal trial protocol and associated statistical analysis plan as an amendment to the Investigational New Drug or IND application, which we expect to submit in the current quarter. This approach eliminates the need for a formal end-of-phase meeting, which may expedite study initiation and registration."
  • Nolan reported continued strong execution across both the adolescent/adult and pediatric REVEAL Phase 1/2 trials, with TSHA-102 demonstrating a favorable safety profile and functional gains in patients. He added, "TSHA-102 continues to be generally well-tolerated across the high and low dose cohorts with no treatment related serious adverse events or dose limiting toxicities as of the April 10, 2025 data cutoff."
  • Sukumar Nagendran, President and Head of R&D, emphasized the importance of aligning clinical endpoints with the FDA, noting, "These functional outcomes seen thus far in patients treated with TSHA-102 have supported our ability to reach alignment with the FDA on key elements of the pivotal trial design for TSHA-102 and next steps to enable study initiation."
  • CFO Kamran Alam stated, "Research and development expenses were $15.6 million for the three months ended March 31, 2025 compared to $20.7 million for the three months ended March 31, 2024. The $5.1 million decrease was driven by Good Manufacturing Practices or GMP batch activities with the intended commercial manufacturing process for TSHA-102 performed during the three months ended March 31, 2024."

Outlook

  • Taysha expects to submit the pivotal trial protocol and statistical analysis plan for TSHA-102 to the FDA as an IND amendment in the current quarter. Management indicated this could expedite the start of the pivotal Part B trial, stating, "We are focused on finalizing the details of our protocols and statistical analysis plan as we prepare to submit the IND amendment this quarter."
  • The company plans to provide a comprehensive update on the pivotal Part B trial design, Rett syndrome natural history analysis, and clinical data from Part A at the International Rett Syndrome Foundation’s 2025 Scientific Meeting in June.
  • No changes were stated in guidance on cash runway, with resources expected to support operations into the fourth quarter of 2026.

Financial Results

  • Alam reported general and administrative expenses were $8.2 million for the quarter, up from $7.1 million a year earlier, primarily due to higher compensation expenses and increased legal and professional fees.
  • Net loss for the quarter was $21.5 million or $0.08 per share, compared to $24.1 million or $0.10 per share for the same period in 2024.
  • Cash and cash equivalents stood at $116.6 million as of March 31, 2025.
  • Management reiterated that "current cash resources will support planned operating expenses and capital requirements into the fourth quarter of 2026."

Q&A

  • Kristen Kluska, Cantor Fitzgerald, asked about signals for dose response in a heterogeneous syndrome. Nolan responded, "There’s a couple of things we can look at. One is, as you mentioned, a temporal aspect... you see early improvements and strengthening over time. We think there could be differentiation at the high dose... assuming the safety continues to look like we’ve seen thus far, we’ve always had the view that the high dose would be the right dose."
  • Lydia for Salveen Richter, Goldman Sachs, inquired about the bar for success and regulatory feedback. Nolan explained, "Number 1, see what the natural history analysis that we’ve conducted highlights... We’ll then explain what the regulatory details are. So, we’ve reached alignment on key framework... on the trial design... the primary endpoint... the study population... and the estimated number of patients."
  • Gil Blum, Needham and Co., asked about potential FDA feedback post-30-day submission period. Nolan stated, "We’ve reached alignment with the FDA on really the key aspects of the trial design... So, really, at this point, it’s putting the detail into the protocol and making that submission."
  • Multiple analysts repeatedly questioned the harmonization of endpoints, trial design, and the significance of functional gains as primary outcomes, all addressed as being aligned with FDA expectations and rooted in the evolving clinical dataset.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts displayed a positive and constructive tone, congratulating the company on regulatory milestones and seeking clarification on trial design, endpoints, and FDA interactions. Questions probed for further detail but did not reflect skepticism or negativity.
  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone throughout both the prepared remarks and Q&A. Nolan frequently referenced productive collaboration with the FDA and the robustness of the clinical data, stating, "We are pleased with the significant regulatory and clinical progress made across our TSHA-102 Rett syndrome program."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analyst and management sentiment remained positive, with the current quarter reflecting increased confidence following written FDA alignment and the removal of an end-of-phase meeting as a regulatory hurdle.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter marked a key transition from ongoing regulatory discussions to formal written alignment with the FDA on pivotal trial design, expediting the timeline to pivotal trial initiation.
  • Analysts in both quarters focused on FDA interactions, trial design, primary endpoints, and data maturity. The latest quarter saw more detailed discussion of the regulatory pathway and the impact of FDA alignment on trial timelines.
  • Management’s tone in the current quarter was more confident, emphasizing expedited processes and regulatory clarity, compared with the prior quarter’s focus on ongoing discussions and data maturation.
  • Financial results reflected a reduction in R&D expenses and net loss compared to the year-ago quarter, with continued assurance on cash runway.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management called out the heterogeneity of Rett syndrome and the need for robust, objective functional outcomes as clinical endpoints.
  • There was no mention of adverse trends in safety, with Nagendran noting, "no treatment emergent adverse events or dose limiting toxicity" as of the April data cutoff.
  • Analysts sought clarity on regulatory timelines, the possibility of FDA feedback following protocol submission, and the role of natural history data in trial design, but no major operational or regulatory risks were newly introduced.

Final Takeaway

Taysha Gene Therapies emphasized the transformative potential of TSHA-102 for Rett syndrome, underpinned by written FDA alignment on pivotal trial design and expedited regulatory processes. With a robust safety profile, meaningful functional gains in treated patients, and a clear regulatory path, the company is positioned to initiate its pivotal Part B trial following protocol submission this quarter, with further clinical and regulatory updates anticipated at the IRSF Scientific Meeting in June. Cash reserves are expected to fund operations into the fourth quarter of 2026, supporting continued advancement of the TSHA-102 program.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Eli Lilly sees Alzheimer's drug development beyond amyloid beta targeting
data.description:
While many pharma and biotech companies developing drugs to treat Alzheimer's disease are targeting amyloid beta plaque, Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) is looking at other targets as well.

The drugmaker's approved treatment, Kisunla (donanemab), works by binding to deposited amyloid plaque in the brain. Its phase 3 Alzheimer's asset, remternetug, also binds to amyloid plaques. It is being eyed for early Alzheimer's and potentially with subcutaneous administration.

Lilly is looking at treating Alzheimer's in earlier stages of the disease, SVP, US Neuroscience, Laura Steele said Thursday at the FT Live US Pharma and Biotech Summit in NYC.

In phase 2, Lilly is examining mevidalen, a small molecule drug that acts as a positive allosteric modulator of the dopamine receptor. It is under investigation for symptomatic treatment.

Steele noted that the company is also looking at the possibility Kisunla could be used as a treatment for earlier stages of Alzheimer's, citing the TRAILBLAZER ALZ-3 trial which is focused on patients that have amyloid plaque but no disease symptoms. A data readout is expected in 2027.

Other Alzheimer's drug candidates that don't target amyloid plaque include Anavex Life Sciences' (AVXL) blarcamesine.

data.title: DigitalBridge gains on report it's in talks with 26North Partners
data.description:
• DigitalBridge (NYSE:DBRG) rose 11% amid a report that 26North Partners is in advanced talks to acquire the digital infrastructure fund manager.
  • Energy trader Mercuria is speculated to be partnering with 26North on an acquisition and a deal may happen within days, according to a report from IJGlobal that was circulating among traders on Thursday. 
  • DigitalBridge (NYSE:DBRG) didn't immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's email request for comment.

data.title: Ondas reaffirms $25M 2025 revenue target while expanding drone and rail platforms
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Ondas Holdings Inc. (ONDS) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Eric Brock opened the call by highlighting "a very positive story to tell today," noting strong momentum for Ondas entering 2025, especially with Ondas Autonomous Systems (OAS). Brock stated that the company generated $4.2 million in revenue in Q1, driven by execution against a $10 million backlog. He announced more than $9 million in additional orders year-to-date, growing the backlog to $16.8 million, and reaffirmed a full-year revenue goal of at least $25 million for 2025, with $20 million expected from OAS. Brock also revealed the addition of a third Iron Drone customer with a $1.7 million order for border security, and shared that the company met its initial goal of adding two new military or homeland security customers in 2025, now raising the target to four for the year.

  • Brock reported that the Iron Drone radar system achieved operational status and that marketing activities, including the demonstration team launched in February, are "generating significant new demand, supported by the Iron Drone's real-world combat readiness." He highlighted expansions in the Optimus Drone fleet in the UAE and ongoing business with new and existing defense and public safety customers in the U.S. and internationally.

  • On the networks side, Brock cited the selection of dot16 by the Association of American Railroads (AAR) as a communications protocol standard for the Next-Generation Head of Train / End of Train system, and the ratification of the 802.16t standard by IEEE as markers of industry validation and strategic progress for Ondas Networks.

  • Interim CFO Neil Laird stated, "Revenues increased over 500% to $4.2 million in Q1 2025 from $0.6 million in Q1 2024." Laird added, "Gross profit was $1.5 million, representing a 35% gross margin in Q1 2025 as compared to a $0.4 million gross loss in Q1 of 2024." He discussed increased operating expenses to $11.8 million and a cash position of $25.4 million as of March 31, 2025, noting "the reduction in debt at the holding company is a significant positive, in my opinion."

  • Brock acknowledged board changes, with Joe Popolo stepping down due to his nomination as U.S. Ambassador to the Netherlands, and addressed the company's new $225 million shelf registration, stating, "We will be sensitive to our cost of capital, which of course is the inverse to our stock price."

Outlook

  • Brock reaffirmed the full-year revenue target of at least $25 million for 2025, led by OAS with at least $20 million in sales. He noted, "We now believe we can add more of these customers via programs this year and have increased our goal from two to four new military or homeland security customers for the full year 2025."

  • The company expects the backlog to grow further in 2025 beyond the current $16.8 million with new orders, and projects sustained or accelerated growth into 2026.

Financial Results

  • Ondas reported Q1 2025 revenues of $4.2 million, with $4.0 million from OAS and $0.2 million from Ondas Networks. Gross profit reached $1.5 million with a 35% margin. Operating expenses were $11.8 million, and the operating loss was $10.3 million. Adjusted EBITDA loss was $7.5 million. The company held $25.4 million in cash and reported $25.4 million in convertible debt outstanding as of March 31, 2025, which was further reduced to $20.6 million in Q2 via conversions.

Q&A

  • Maxwell Michaelis, Lake Street Capital Markets: Asked about the size and frequency of follow-on orders for drone customers. Brock responded, "The initial orders are often pilot programs in nature. We work to install and operate the system... we're in parallel making plans for significant expansions, I would say."
  • Michaelis followed up on NATO customer discussions. Brock confirmed, "Yes, absolutely. There's been a significant shift in policies on defense spending... We see ourselves as major beneficiaries."
  • Glenn Mattson, Ladenburg Thalmann: Inquired about growth for Optimus in 2025 and U.S. opportunities. Brock said, "We do have existing customers that will expand programs... We're targeting those markets, right? Public safety, it's security, homeland security and defense, and the protection of critical infrastructure assets."
  • Mattson asked about funding sources for U.S. deals. Brock replied, "It's really both, and it depends on where we're going... typically going to be funded by state and local governments... On the federal side, we see quite a bit of that."
  • Mattson asked about Middle East defense deals. Brock responded, "Yes, I absolutely believe we can be part of those conversations. But I guess I'd have to leave it at that."
  • Matthew Galinko, Maxim Group: Queried on margin profile for Iron Drone orders. Brock stated, "You should think of our systems as having both Optimus and Iron Drone margins north of 50%. And I would say that Iron Drone margins are better than Optimus."
  • Galinko asked about tariff risks and supply chain impacts. Brock answered, "We're very careful to source away from China. We design our platforms to be NDA compliant... I would think that for us, the direct and indirect impacts are going to be manageable."
  • Mike Latimore, Northland Capital Markets: Asked about the OAS pipeline mix between expansions and new customers. Brock said, "There’re significant expansion opportunities with new logos... I expect a significant new customer program growth in 2025, 2026 and beyond."
  • Latimore queried about manufacturing capacity for Optimus and Iron Drone. Brock replied, "We have ample capacity to hit our plans in Israel... we're going to build the capacity in the United States."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed optimism about growth, order momentum, and market opportunities, but also probed for clarity on order expansion, funding sources, margin profiles, and tariff exposure, signaling a slightly positive to neutral tone overall.
  • Management maintained a confident and constructive tone throughout, emphasizing growth, operational discipline, and market validation. Brock repeatedly underscored the company's momentum, stating, "We are executing well against our strategy, expanding our production and service capabilities to scale with demand."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management conveyed more confidence and clarity regarding backlog, customer expansion, and margin improvements.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Management reaffirmed the $25 million revenue target for 2025 and increased its goal for new military or homeland security customers from two to four, signaling heightened confidence in market traction.
  • The backlog grew from $10 million entering 2025 to $16.8 million in Q1, reflecting increased order activity.
  • OAS revenue continued to be the main driver, while Ondas Networks saw steady but modest revenue, with validation from industry standards and ongoing rail deployments.
  • The leadership transition at OAS and Ondas Networks was completed in Q1, with new CEOs in place for both units.
  • Analyst focus shifted further toward customer expansion, margin profiles, and operational execution, while management provided more details on debt reduction and capital strategy.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management flagged ongoing sensitivity around classified customer activities, particularly for defense and homeland security customers.
  • Tariff risk was discussed as manageable, with Brock stating, "We're very careful to source away from China... the direct and indirect impacts are going to be manageable."
  • The low conversion price of convertible notes was acknowledged as a negative, with Brock attributing it to "a significant decline in our share price despite what we believe is a significantly improving company fundamentals."
  • Operating losses and cash utilization remain key areas of focus, though management expects improved efficiency and leverage as revenues grow.

Final Takeaway

Ondas Holdings enters 2025 with accelerating momentum, a growing backlog, and reaffirmed confidence in its revenue and customer expansion targets. The company is leveraging validated technology platforms in both defense drone and rail communications markets, supported by record order activity and expanding partnerships. Management maintains a disciplined approach to growth and capital deployment, expecting 2025 to be a breakout year with continued operational and financial progress.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Coinbase user numbers come under SEC scrutiny - report
data.description:
The Securities and Exchange Commission has been investigating whether crypto exchange Coinbase Global (NASDAQ:COIN) misstated its user numbers in past financial statements, according to a media report.

The probe into a metric the company calls "verified users" started during the Biden administration and has continued under President Trump's, the New York Times said, citing four people familiar with the investigation. Coinbase (NASDAQ:COIN) used the measure in its original public offering document in 2021, but stopped disclosing it two years later.

While the SEC has dropped a number of lawsuits against crypto firms after the change in administration, it shows that the regulator hasn't completely ended scrutiny of the sector. In February, the SEC dismissed its civil lawsuit against Coinbase (COIN) that alleged it was an unregistered securities broker.

"This is a hold-over investigation from the prior administration about a metric we stopped reporting two and a half years ago, which was fully disclosed to the public," Coinbase's (COIN) chief legal officer, Paul Grewal, said in an emailed statement to Seeking Alpha. He said the company explained the term  when it used the measure, and continues to disclose what it considers a more relevant metric — monthly transacting users.

"While we strongly believe this investigation should not continue, we remain committed to working with the SEC to bring this matter to a close," he added.

Coinbase (COIN) defines verified users as "all retail users, institutions, and ecosystem partners that have registered an account on our platform and confirmed either their email address or phone number, or that have established an account with a username on our non-custodial wallet application, as of the date of measurement."

Coinbase (COIN) stock dropped 6.0% in Thursday afternoon trading, even as bitcoin (BTC-USD) rsoe 0.6% to $104.1K.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Stantec projects 7%-10% net revenue growth and maintains optimism amid record $7.9B backlog
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Stantec Inc. (STN) Q1 2025

Management View

  • President and CEO Gord Johnston highlighted a strong start to 2025, citing organic growth across all regional and business operating units, with Canada achieving double-digit organic growth. Johnston stated, "Stantec had a very strong start to 2025, delivering organic growth in each of our regional and business operating units, most notably in Canada with double-digit organic growth." Net revenue reached $1.6B, up 13.3% year-over-year, underpinned by 5.9% organic and 3.2% acquisition growth. Johnston also emphasized the completion of two strategic acquisitions: Page, a 1,400-person architecture and engineering firm in Washington, D.C., and Ryan Hanley, a 150-person engineering and environmental consultancy in Ireland. Johnston noted, "The acquisition of Page will deepen Stantec's expertise and resources in key growth areas, such as advanced manufacturing, data centers and health care, while adding new capabilities in cleanroom design and fabrication facilities." He indicated the Page acquisition is expected to close in Q3.

  • Executive Vice President and CFO Vito Culmone reported, "Our gross revenue in Q1 grew to $1.9 billion, up almost 12% year-over-year, and net revenue of $1.6 billion is up 13.3% compared to Q1 of 2024." Culmone also stated, "We achieved a very solid adjusted EBITDA margin of 16.2% in the quarter, representing an increase of 70 basis points year-over-year. And our adjusted EPS in the quarter increased almost 29% to $1.16."

Outlook

  • CEO Johnston stated, "We are maintaining our current outlook, which includes net revenue growth of 7% to 10% for the year, EBITDA margin in the range of 16.7% to 17.3% ... and adjusted EPS growth to be in the range of 16% to 19%, once again, above our net revenue growth expectations." Management kept its guidance unchanged, awaiting the closure of acquisitions and further clarity from Q2 performance.

  • Johnston reiterated the company’s confidence in achieving its 2024-2026 strategic plan targets, stating, "I'm confident that we can achieve our strategic plan target of 50% growth in net revenues to $7.5 billion by the end of next year."

Financial Results

  • Stantec delivered net revenue of $1.6B, an increase of 13.3% year-over-year, and gross revenue of $1.9B, up almost 12%. Adjusted EBITDA margin improved to 16.2% from last year, and adjusted EPS reached $1.16, up nearly 29%. Operating cash flow saw substantial growth to $101M from $43M in the prior year, and the net debt to adjusted EBITDA ratio was 1.1x at quarter end.

  • Project margins as a percentage of net revenue reached 54.3%, a 10-basis-point increase over the prior year. The backlog hit a new company record at $7.9B, growing nearly 13% year-over-year, with 7.5% organic growth.

Q&A

  • Benoit Poirier, Desjardins: Asked about U.S. organic growth and guidance discipline. CEO Johnston explained that the 2.4% organic growth in the U.S. was impacted by a tough comparison to last year’s double-digit performance and the completion of a significant water project, but expressed confidence in reaching mid- to high-single-digit growth by year-end.

  • Chris Murray, ATB Capital Markets: Inquired about U.S. government business uncertainty and integration of ZETCON in Germany. Johnston noted Stantec had not seen appreciable impact from government uncertainty and said, "the integration of ZETCON, we're taking it a little bit slower ... we're maintaining that philosophy."

  • Krista Friesen, CIBC: Asked about M&A pipeline size and focus. Johnston stated the company is comfortable with both small and larger deals and sees opportunities in water, infrastructure, environmental, and energy & resources.

  • Michael Tupholme, TD Cowen: Questioned macro uncertainty’s impact on M&A and the turnaround in Energy & Resources. Johnston said, "we just continue to take that long-term approach" and highlighted 8.3% organic growth in E&R this quarter.

  • Maxim Sytchev, NBF: Queried infrastructure demand in Canada and Australia. Johnston sees continued support and strengthening in both markets, with Australia’s water sector noted as "really, really strong."

  • Devin Dodge, BMO Capital Markets: Asked about data center market exposure. Johnston responded, "that represents 2% to 3% of our overall revenue," providing insulation against broader market swings.

  • Jonathan Goldman, Scotiabank: Asked about margin sustainability. Culmone said, "I would look at Q1 and consider that an indication of the pacing of the business, for sure."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts’ tone was neutral to slightly positive, probing for details on growth sustainability, integration, and M&A pipeline scale, with recurring questions on U.S. growth, margin sustainability, and M&A discipline.

  • Management maintained a confident tone throughout both prepared remarks and Q&A, reiterating guidance and expressing optimism. Johnston repeatedly emphasized confidence in achieving strategic targets and navigating industry uncertainty. The sentiment matches that of the previous quarter, with no notable increase in defensiveness.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst skepticism over guidance and margin sustainability was relatively unchanged, while management's composure and confidence remained steady.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Stantec maintained its guidance for net revenue growth (7%-10%), EBITDA margin (16.7%-17.3%), and adjusted EPS growth (16%-19%) from the previous quarter.

  • Strategic focus on M&A accelerated, with two key acquisitions announced in Q1 and expectations for more. The Page acquisition is expected to make Stantec the second largest architectural firm in North America.

  • Backlog reached a record $7.9B in Q1, up from $7.8B at year-end, showing continued strong demand and execution.

  • Management’s tone and confidence level in the outlook and operational execution were consistent with Q4, with no significant change in language or emphasis. Analyst questions continued to center on growth sustainability and M&A discipline, similar to Q4.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited "heightened market uncertainty driven by tariffs, policy shifts and regulatory changes," but expressed confidence in meeting outlook targets.

  • Johnston referenced "a little bit of slowing in procurement cycles" in U.S. government work, but noted a return to normal cadence.

  • No material impact from macro uncertainty on project awards, M&A, or private sector client activity was reported.

Final Takeaway

Stantec’s management emphasized a robust start to 2025, underpinned by double-digit growth in Canada, strong M&A momentum, record backlog, and sustained profitability improvement. The company maintained its full-year guidance and reiterated confidence in achieving strategic growth targets, supported by continued demand across geographies and sectors. Management highlighted a strong balance sheet, a full M&A pipeline, and resilience in the face of macroeconomic uncertainty, expressing optimism for continued growth and shareholder value creation through 2025 and beyond.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Altria declares $1.02 dividend

• Altria (NYSE:MO) declares $1.02/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 7.08%
  • Payable July 10; for shareholders of record June 16; ex-div June 16.
